title: How LLMs Actually Work
summary: Tokenization, embeddings, attention, decoding. The mental model before the hype.

title: Tokenization: Where the Bugs Live
summary: BPE, SentencePiece, tiktoken. Context windows are measured in tokens, not words, and half the subtle bugs live here.

title: Attention: The Core Primitive
summary: Scaled dot-product, multi-head, positional encodings, causal masks. The mechanics, not the paper (see attention-is-all-you-need).

title: Training vs Inference: Different Problems
summary: Pretraining, fine-tuning, RLHF, inference-time compute. Three regimes, three cost profiles, three failure modes.

title: Context Windows and Why They Matter
summary: From 2K to 10M tokens. What changes, what doesn't, where long-context retrieval quietly breaks.

title: Prompting as Programming
summary: System prompts, few-shot, chain-of-thought. Treating prompts as code artifacts.

title: Before Transformers (2012-2017)
summary: AlexNet, word2vec, seq2seq, LSTMs. The ML world transformers replaced.

title: Transformers to GPT-3 (2017-2020)
summary: Attention Is All You Need, then BERT, then GPT-2, then GPT-3. Where scaling laws first click.

title: The ChatGPT Era (2022-2023)
summary: InstructGPT, RLHF, the consumer breakout. 100M users in two months.

title: The Reasoning Turn (2024-2025)
summary: Sep 2024 o1, then Claude 3.5 extended thinking, then DeepSeek-R1 (Jan 2025). RL on chain-of-thought becomes the frontier recipe.

title: The Agentic Era (2026)
summary: Tool use, long-horizon tasks, multi-agent orchestration. Write code that writes code as the default dev interface.

title: Frontier Labs: Who's Actually Shipping
summary: OpenAI ($852B, consumer mindshare), Anthropic ($380B, passed OpenAI in revenue on the back of Constitutional AI and interpretability), Google DeepMind (TPU vertical integration), Meta FAIR (open weights, Llama 4), xAI (Memphis Colossus).

title: The Open-Weights Camp
summary: Llama 4, Mistral, DeepSeek V3.2 and R1, Qwen 3.5. Why open weights matter strategically, and how the Chinese labs reset the training is expensive narrative.

title: Chinese Labs and the Capability Gap
summary: DeepSeek, Qwen/Alibaba, Zhipu, Moonshot (Kimi). Speed of iteration, export-control arbitrage, the DeepSeek-R1 moment.

title: Infrastructure Players
summary: NVIDIA (still 80%+ of training compute), TSMC, hyperscaler capex. Challengers: AMD MI300X, Groq, Cerebras, SambaNova, Google TPUs.

title: Frontier Models (Q1 2026)
summary: GPT-5.4, Claude Opus 4.6, Gemini 3.1 Pro, Grok 4. The frontier four, and what each is actually best at. Leaderboard specifics live in benchmarks-that-matter.

title: Benchmarks That Still Matter
summary: SWE-bench Verified (~80%), ARC-AGI v2, GPQA near saturation, FrontierMath still <3%. What new evals catch that MMLU cannot.

title: The Multimodal Frontier
summary: Native image/audio/video understanding, Sora and Veo, Genie world models. Where modalities merge into one latent.

title: Long-Horizon Tasks
summary: What hours-long autonomous work actually means in Q1 2026, and where it breaks (context rot, tool drift, silent failures).

title: Interpretability and SAEs
summary: Sparse autoencoders, feature steering, circuit analysis. Why this stopped being academic-only: production safety filters, jailbreak detection, real model debugging.

title: Attention Is All You Need (2017)
summary: The 2017 paper itself: what it claimed, what it got right, what aged oddly (sinusoidal positional encodings, the original 6-layer depth).

title: Scaling Laws (Kaplan 2020, Chinchilla 2022)
summary: Kaplan's first quantitative curves, then Chinchilla rewrites them: compute-optimal means data matters more than params.

title: InstructGPT / RLHF (2022)
summary: How alignment tuning turned raw GPT-3 into a product surface. The paper behind ChatGPT.

title: Constitutional AI (Anthropic 2022)
summary: RLAIF, harmlessness at scale, the Anthropic house style.

title: ReAct: The Foundational Agent Loop (2023)
summary: Reason plus act plus observe as the base loop every tool-using agent inherits.

title: Must-Read Papers (2025)
summary: Scaling Monosemanticity, o1 System Card, DeepSeek-R1, Gemini 1.5 long-context technical report. The short list a 2025 practitioner actually cites.

title: RLHF, DPO, and Friends
summary: PPO (InstructGPT), DPO (no reward model needed), KTO, RLAIF, Constitutional AI. Preference-optimization family, what each solves.

title: Mixture of Experts
summary: Why sparse beats dense at a given compute budget. Routing problems, Mixtral and DeepSeek's MoE lessons.

title: Inference-Time Compute
summary: Search, self-consistency, speculative decoding, budget controllers. The dial that turns capability up at the cost of latency. (RL-on-CoT lives in reasoning-turn.)

title: Tool Use and Function Calling
summary: The mechanism behind every agentic workflow. MCP, OpenAPI-style schemas, what the model actually sees.

title: RAG in Practice
summary: Chunking, embeddings, vector stores, hybrid search, reranking, context stuffing. What actually works once the demo ends.

title: Observability and Evals
summary: LangSmith, Braintrust, Helicone, Phoenix. Trace inspection, eval pipelines, regression detection. The infra layer that makes agentic systems debuggable in prod.

title: Handoff Protocol Between Agents
summary: Most multi-agent failure I've seen comes from sloppy handoff. Here's the four-block contract.

title: Failure Modes in Multi-Agent Systems
summary: Where agent teams quietly break: context drift, mock divergence, and unowned escalations.

title: Agentic Workflows: Landscape 2026
summary: State of the art in agentic workflows, Q1 2026: what's working, what's overhyped, what's next.

title: Orchestrator vs Swarm: A Taste Test
summary: Two dominant multi-agent paradigms, observed failure profiles, which I actually ship.

title: Agent Economies Landscape
summary: Structures, sandboxes, crypto economies, and protocols for autonomous agent markets.

title: Capex and the GPU Market
summary: Hyperscaler capex around $300B in 2025-26, NVIDIA still 80%+ of training. Blackwell (B200) to Rubin. TSMC as the gating factor.

title: AI Revenue: Signal vs Narrative
summary: Anthropic ($30B ARR) passed OpenAI ($25B) in Q1 2026 despite 4x less training spend. 80% enterprise mix. OpenAI burns $17B/year.

title: The Token Price Collapse
summary: Cost-per-token fell roughly 90% in 2024 and kept dropping. What it enables (agent workloads), who it pressures (inference margin).
